abstract.t: This document specifies the High-Frequency Action-Integrity Protocol (HAIP), a standard for real-time identity verification and autonomous resilience in AI agent environments. HAIP addresses the "Trust Tax" and the "Integrity Gap" by mandating a 6.42 us pulse, decentralized identity teleportation, and NIST-aligned non-repudiation.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: As the AI economy shifts from "Human-to-AI" to "Agent-to-Agent" workflows, the primary security threat is Identity Spoofing. In this environment, identity is the only perimeter. HAIP provides a unified Root of Trust to secure these interactions at scale.

section.name: Terminology and Requirements Language
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in RFC 2119.

li.t: Pulse: A micro-signed cryptographic heartbeat emitted by an agent.

section.name: The Integrity Pulse Window
section.t: HAIP-compliant agents MUST emit a state-hash "Heartbeat" at a frequency defined by the Registry (default: 100ms). The Registry MUST verify this pulse within 6.42 us to mitigate Time-of-Check (TOC) window exploits.

section.name: Pulse Packet Structure
section.t: The Pulse packet MUST follow a structured format containing the Sovereign ID, pulse type, state hash, timestamp, and Ed25519 signature.

section.name: Financial Integrity: Zero-Variance Math
section.t: HAIP integrates an integer-based settlement layer where $1.00 USD is treated as 10,000 "Pulse Units" to eliminate floating-point rounding drift.

section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: This document requests the allocation of Port 5001 for HAIP Heartbeat synchronization.

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: HAIP strictly mandates an Action-Integrity Threshold (Tai) of <= 1,000 us to mitigate Flash-Injection attacks.
